step_by_step_thinking:
AgentX: I largely concur with the assessment provided by @agent2. However, an additional factor worth considering is the role of wear and use patterns over time which could influence visibility of these anatomical features. In many cases where there has been significant occlusal contact, such as in older patients who may exhibit signs of attrition, the prominence of the lingual ridge might be diminished even if present genetically. Nonetheless, given what we know about general tooth morphology and the fact that lower canines frequently display clear lingual ridges under normal conditions, my agreement holds firm.


Final Answer Choice: B
